clips.title: Leaving for the Day
clips.about: Describe a person getting ready and leaving the house on a sunny morning.
lines: It is a bright morning, and a person stands near the front door.
'It is' describes the weather and time of day.
First, the person puts on a light jacket.
'First' shows the beginning of the sequence; 'put on' means to wear clothes.
Then he picks up a bag and some keys from the table.
'Then' links to the next action; 'pick up' means to lift something in your hand.
After that, he looks up at the round clock on the wall.
'After that' continues the sequence; 'look up at' means to raise your eyes toward something.
Finally, he opens the door and steps out into the sunny street.
'Finally' shows the last action; 'step out' means to go outside.

checks.q: What does the person put on first?
opts: a light jacket
The narration says he puts on a light jacket first.
a warm hat
There is no hat in the scene.
a pair of shoes
Shoes are not mentioned in the narration.
checks.why: The first action after standing by the door is putting on a light jacket.

checks.q: What does he do at the end of the clip?
opts: He opens the door and goes outside.
The last line says he opens the door and steps out into the street.
He sits down on a chair.
He leaves the house; he does not sit down.
He puts the keys back on the table.
He picks up the keys; he does not put them back.
checks.why: In the final action he opens the door and steps out into the sunny street.

clips.title: A Table at the Café
clips.about: Describe a person receiving and enjoying a coffee at a cozy café.
lines: A person sits at a small table in a warm, cozy café.
'Sits at' describes where someone is; present tense shows what is happening now.
First, a waiter gently sets down a cup of coffee.
'First' begins the sequence; 'set down' means to put something on a surface.
Then the waiter puts a small plate with a pastry next to the cup.
'Then' adds the next step; 'next to' shows position.
After that, the person nods to say thank you and stirs the coffee.
'After that' continues the sequence; 'nod' means to move your head up and down.
Finally, she looks around the room and enjoys the quiet café.
'Finally' marks the last action; 'look around' means to turn and see many things.

checks.q: What does the waiter bring to the table?
opts: a coffee and a pastry
The waiter sets down a cup of coffee and a small plate with a pastry.
a glass of water
No water is shown in the scene.
a bowl of soup
There is no soup in the narration.
checks.why: The waiter brings a cup of coffee and a small plate with a pastry.

checks.q: How does the person say thank you?
opts: She nods her head.
The narration says the person nods to say thank you.
She waves goodbye.
She nods; she does not wave goodbye.
She claps her hands.
Clapping is not shown in the scene.
checks.why: The person nods to thank the waiter, because no one speaks in the clip.
